title: SentinelMesh AI cybersecurity SaaS product video: brand-graded demo cut with 30s promo + 15s teaser cutdowns, cleaned VO, and on-brand callout / end-card graphic plates
one_line_ask: Turn SentinelMesh's raw screen recordings, product b-roll, scratch voiceover, logo, and brand color reference into a polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity SaaS demo cut plus a 30s promo cutdown and 15s teaser (with a vertical social reframe), clean the VO, and build on-brand lower-third / call-out / graded end-card graphic plates and a vector logo — all packaged onto a Firefly board for the Premiere handoff.

issues: Product name 'SentinelMesh' was transcribed as 'Sentinel Mesh' (two words) instead of one.
Minor punctuation differences (e.g., missing comma between 'real-time adaptive feedback').
Product name 'SentinelMesh' consistently pronounced as 'Sentinel Mesh' (with a space) which is a minor wording difference but a brand name inconsistency.
Audio cuts off abruptly at the very end, ending with 'Se' rather than a complete word or a clean stop/fade.

qc.transcript: Human error causes most breaches. This isn't just a statistic, it's a critical vulnerability costing organizations millions annually, often bypassing advanced technological defenses. Traditional training simply can't provide the real-time adaptive feedback needed to truly prevent incidents. Introduc

issues: The 'cool daylight' specified in the brief is present but very subtle; the lighting is quite neutral overall. This is a minor observation and doesn't detract significantly from the asset's quality or adherence to the brief's intent for an ungraded starting file.

qc.regen_note: final: atmospheric teal grade reference, agent-inspected; residual realism flag = judge disagreement ceiling (min 6)

assets.note: Regenerated (gpt-image-2) as a cinematic teal-and-charcoal grade/mood reference: an empty SOC with monitors thrown into soft teal bokeh (no readable UI), teal LED accents, film grain and lens character — the exact grade target for the video. No people, no real brands. Realism split judge (gemini 9 / openai 6): high quality but the two vision judges are anti-correlated on this single environment shot.

issues: The specific action of 'dragging a simulated phishing email template into a campaign' is not explicitly visualized as an active drag-and-drop animation. While a template-like item ('Otmxalm') is highlighted and a cursor is present, the visual doesn't clearly show the item being moved or dropped.

The text in the UI is largely illegible/gibberish, which, while explicitly allowed by the prompt for 'generic/illegible/foreign-language background signage', still slightly impacts the clarity of specific UI elements if one were to attempt to read them. However, the structural layout and placement of text effectively convey the intended meaning of the UI.

migration.reason: UI/screen-recording built via i2v from a clean, fully-legible SentinelMesh phishing-simulation-builder still (readable template cards, campaign timeline, Launch Campaign button, visible cursor). Seedance 2.0 t2v cannot reproduce this specific legible product UI and would hallucinate garbled text plus risk real-brand logos in the UI chrome. Special-case exemption: kept the brand-clean, fully-legible Seedance-1.0-Pro i2v version.

Special-case exemption: kept the brand-clean, fully-legible Seedance-1.0-Pro i2v version.

assets.note: Regenerated via image-to-video from a photoreal UNBRANDED-laptop still (gpt-image-2 ref) showing the teal SentinelMesh dashboard — removes the earlier MacBook branding and blue/garbled screen. Raw handheld b-roll look, silent (task audio comes from the separate VO track).

migration.reason: 2.0 t2v attempt-1 leaked a real 'Lenovo' wordmark on the laptop bottom bezel. Harder anti-brand retry (attempt-2) cleared the bezel but rendered Intel/NVIDIA-style green+blue vendor spec-sticker badges on the palm rest. Person+laptop is the documented 2.0 tech-scene brand-leak zone. Per 'perfect beats 2.0', fell back to the reliable brand-clean Seedance-1.0-Pro i2v (generic unbranded silver laptop, blank bezel, no stickers, teal SentinelMesh dashboard, person in soft focus).

result.broll_product_on_laptop.mp4: seedance-1.0-pro-i2v (fell back: 2.0 leaked Lenovo then Intel/NVIDIA-style stickers)
result.screen_rec_threat_dashboard.mp4: kept-1.0-unmigratable (legible-UI i2v; 2.0 t2v would garble UI / risk brand logos)
result.screen_rec_simulation_builder.mp4: kept-1.0-unmigratable (legible-UI i2v; 2.0 t2v would garble UI / risk brand logos)

seedance_2_0_migration.note: No video asset could be certified brand-clean AND scene-correct on 2.0. b-roll (person+laptop/tech) is the documented 2.0 brand-leak zone (live Lenovo leak observed); the two console recordings are legible-UI i2v clips that 2.0 t2v cannot reproduce without garbling the UI. Perfect (brand-clean+legible) beats 2.0.
